other_observers: I cannot recall the name of the man with me; I do not know who found these birds originally, either the first year they wintered or upon their return in this winter
specific_location: Bud Mielke Reservoir, west of Loveland
description: These were all adult swans -- very large birds with all-white plumage and very long necks that they held essentially erect, that is, with no kink at the base, as in Tundra Swan (TUSW). The bills were all dark except for salmon-colored cutting edges. The meeting of bill and feathering was angled diagonally throughout, without the vertical aspect of that meeting on TUSWs. There was no yellow on the bills of any of the birds. They dark eyes were not isolated from the bills of the birds as on TUSWs.Behaviors: Foraging; the behaviors of the birds suggested that they were comprised of two mated pairs.Call: None heardPlumage: Adult
